campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall spending. The council also discussed plans to improve how people get around Campbell using different types of transportation like buses, bikes, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city money management
Monthly spending and treasurer's financial report
New transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and pedestrian options in Campbell

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council fast-tracked the approval process by skipping the standard first reading and planning to vote on the final adoption at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a new ordinance
Council waived the first reading to speed up the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to speak before council action

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from their March meetings and authorize a construction contract. The main business was reviewing and signing off on minutes from several previous meetings, plus giving the Town Manager permission to hire QLM, Inc. for a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes from six March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan upcoming agenda items for their April 21st and April 28th meetings. The council reviewed what new items to add to each agenda, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked whether any agenda items needed special notice requirements. They also reviewed public records from early April and touched on VEBA Advisory Committee matters.
keyTopics: Added new items to April 21st meeting agenda
Set specific times for certain agenda items to ensure meetings stay on schedule
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
Addressed VEBA Advisory Committee business
Checked notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to plan the city's spending on infrastructure projects for the next fiscal year and to recognize both Teen Month and National Volunteer Week. The council reviewed what capital improvements—like road repairs, park upgrades, and public facilities—the city should invest in during 2026-27.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for infrastructure projects and capital improvements
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City planning for public facilities and community investments

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to approve previous meeting minutes and introduce a new rule about tenant protections in Sunnyvale. The proposed change would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing rules, though specific details about what protections would change weren't provided in this agenda summary.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance on residential tenant protections
Updates to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents had the opportunity to speak about any city issues for up to 3 minutes each. The council also elected new leadership positions (Chair and Vice Chair) and discussed budget planning for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could speak on any city matter
Chair and Vice Chair elections for council leadership
Fiscal year budget development and planning
Note: Council cannot make decisions on issues brought up during public comment unless they're officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for residents. If you disagree with certain city decisions, you have 90 days from the date the decision is final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, or you'll lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for City Council decisions
Civil procedure rules governing resident lawsuits against the city

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and new work requests from individual council members. The council also considered changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed meeting schedules and minutes.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan update for housing and community development programs
New work projects requested by council members
Changes to the council vacancy filling process
Meeting calendars and administrative approvals

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The property owner is asking to officially designate the building as historically important, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and strengthening the building against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for rehabilitation
Seismic safety improvements to existing building
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown Commercial zone development rules
